question_text: Verify the address of the emergency. What is the exact address?
What is the callback number you are calling from?
Tell me exactly what happened.
How old is the patient?
Is the scene safe for you to approach?
Is s/he conscious?
Is s/he breathing normally?
How far did s/he fall? What surface did s/he land on?
Did s/he fall from a standing height, or from an elevated surface like a ladder or roof?
What part of the body hit first?
Did s/he hit the head?
Did s/he lose consciousness at any point, even briefly?
Is s/he confused or not making sense?
Is there any bleeding? Where and how much?
Is the bleeding controlled or still actively flowing?
Can s/he move all arms and legs?
Does s/he have any numbness or tingling in the arms or legs?
Is there pain in the neck or back?
Is there any obvious deformity to any limb? Does anything look bent wrong?
Is there swelling at the injury site?
Can s/he bear weight or stand?
Is s/he on blood thinners like warfarin or Eliquis?
Does s/he have any medical conditions — diabetes, seizures, or a heart condition?
Did s/he faint or feel dizzy before the fall?
Has s/he vomited since the fall?
Is there any fluid coming from the ears or nose?
ECHO-level: Unconscious and not breathing after fall
DELTA-level: Unconscious after fall from significant height
DELTA-level: Uncontrolled hemorrhage — blood pooling or spurting
DELTA-level: Suspected spinal injury with neurological deficit
condition: spinal pain AND numbness/tingling OR motor deficit

question_text: DELTA-level: Open skull fracture or fluid from ears/nose after head impact
condition: open skull fracture OR CSF leak

question_text: CHARLIE-level: Head injury with brief LOC, now conscious and oriented
condition: head injury AND brief LOC AND conscious

question_text: CHARLIE-level: Fall on blood thinners with any head impact
CHARLIE-level: Obvious long bone deformity (femur, humerus)
BRAVO-level: Fall with isolated limb injury, no head or spinal involvement
BRAVO-level: Elderly fall with hip pain, unable to bear weight
condition: elderly AND hip pain AND non-weight-bearing

question_text: BRAVO-level: Controlled bleeding with moderate pain
ALPHA-level: Minor fall from standing, small abrasion or bruise, ambulatory
condition: minor fall AND ambulatory AND minor injury

question_text: ALPHA-level: Isolated ankle or wrist injury, no deformity, mild swelling
condition: isolated extremity AND mild swelling AND no deformity

question_text: OMEGA-level: Fall with no injury, no pain, no head impact, fully ambulatory
condition: no injury AND no pain AND ambulatory

question_text: Do NOT move the patient if there is any possibility of a neck or back injury.
If there is serious bleeding, apply firm direct pressure with a clean cloth. Do not remove the cloth — add more on top if it soaks through.
If a limb looks deformed, do not try to straighten it. Keep it still and supported.
If the patient hit their head, do not let them fall asleep. Keep talking to them.
Apply ice wrapped in a cloth to any swollen area. 20 minutes on, 20 minutes off.
If the patient becomes unconscious and stops breathing, begin CPR.
Keep the patient warm with a blanket. Do not give food or drink.
Stay on the line. Help is on the way.
If anything changes — if s/he becomes confused, starts vomiting, or stops breathing — call us back immediately.
